title: Figure 1d from: Kaitetzidou E, Markomanolaki H, Fabiano F, Paone C, Chatzinikolaou E, Dailianis T (2026) Population-level transcriptomic datasets from two benthic invertebrates exposed to long-term experimental warming and acidification. Biodiversity Data Journal 14: e186927. https://doi.org/10.3897/BDJ.14.e186927
author: Kaitetzidou, Elisavet
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1d Evaluation of RNA integrity and quality using agarose gel electrophoresis and TapeStation analysis. The 28S rRNA band (in the agarose gel image, C) and the corresponding peak (at the TapeStation report image, D) are absent in H. trunculus samples due to the fragility of the 28S rRNA, which is prone to degradation during RNA extraction steps: - H. trunculus RNA analysed using TapeStation.
steps_raw: Figure 1d Evaluation of RNA integrity and quality using agarose gel electrophoresis and TapeStation analysis. The 28S rRNA band (in the agarose gel image, C) and the corresponding peak (at the TapeStation report image, D) are absent in H. trunculus samples due to the fragility of the 28S rRNA, which is prone to degradation during RNA extraction steps: - H. trunculus RNA analysed using TapeStation.
